content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] UI/UX: Search input still has only a placeholder, no accessible name
## Step: review

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: medium
File: src/app.ts:71-73
Title: Search input still has only a placeholder, no accessible name

Finding:
The prior medium finding was not addressed. src/app.ts:71-73 still constructs the search input with only `searchInput.type = 'text'` and `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...'` — there is no associated `<label>`, `aria-label`, or `title`. A placeholder is not an accessible name: it is not reliably exposed to assistive technology and disappears once the user types, leaving the field unlabeled. Screen-reader users get no clear purpose for the field. This code is part of the modified src/app.ts and remains unchanged.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app.ts where the search input is constructed (around lines 71-73), add an accessible name so the field is announced clearly by screen readers and remains labeled after the user types. The simplest fix: after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` add `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');`. Do not rely on the placeholder alone. Alternatively, add a visually-hidden `<label for>` element, but an aria-label is sufficient and consistent with the aria-label pattern used elsewhere (e.g. the dialog close button already sets aria-label). No test changes required, but if a test pins the input attributes, update it to expect the accessible name.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The finding was not addressed. The task explicitly required adding an accessible name to the search input in src/app.ts (around lines 71-73), but the file is unchanged: the input is still constructed with only `searchInput.type = 'text'` and `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...'`. There is no `aria-label`, no `title`, and no associated `<label>` element. The upload input gets a visible `<label>` (uploadLabel wrapping it), but the search input is left with only a placeholder, which is not an accessible name and disappears once the user types. The fix is a one-line addition: `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` immediately after the placeholder assignment, consistent with the existing aria-label pattern already used for the dialog close button (`closeBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Close')`). No test pins these input attributes, so no test update is required. Please apply the targeted fix and re-submit.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }
